body:
This PR fixes a bug in the definitions of `ConformalCubedSpherePanel` and `ConformalCubedSpherePanelGrid` by removing an extra sixth `:Any` type parameter that does not belong in the `OrthogonalSphericalShellGrid` type.

Previously:
```
const ConformalCubedSpherePanel = OrthogonalSphericalShellGrid{<:Any, FullyConnected, FullyConnected,
                                                               <:Any, <:Any, <:Any,
                                                               <:CubedSphereConformalMapping}

const ConformalCubedSpherePanelGrid = OrthogonalSphericalShellGrid{<:Any,
                                                                   <:Any,
                                                                   <:Any,
                                                                   <:Any,
                                                                   <:Any,
                                                                   <:Any,
                                                                   <:CubedSphereConformalMapping}
```
The sixth `:<Any` parameter was unnecessary and did not correspond to any field in the underlying `OrthogonalSphericalShellGrid` type. This PR removes that extraneous type to ensure correctness and proper method dispatch.
title: Fix incorrect type parameter count in ConformalCubedSpherePanel and ConformalCubedSpherePanelGrid definitions

body:
This PR addresses and prevents out-of-bounds memory access during the computation of the vertical vorticity field on the conformal cubed sphere grid, specifically along the southernmost and westernmost halos of each panel.

To reproduce the issue, consider the following minimal working example (MWE) using a Julia session with bounds checking enabled (`--check-bounds=yes`):
```julia
using Oceananigans

arch = CPU()
grid = ConformalCubedSphereGrid(arch; panel_size=(4, 4, 1), z=(-1, 0), horizontal_direction_halo=2, radius=1)
u = XFaceField(grid); set!(u, 1)
v = YFaceField(grid); set!(v, 2)
ζ = Field{Face, Face, Center}(grid)

using KernelAbstractions: @kernel, @index
using Oceananigans.Operators: ζ₃ᶠᶠᶜ

@kernel function _compute_vorticity!(grid, u, v, ζ)
    i, j, k = @index(Global, NTuple)
    @inbounds ζ[i, j, k] = ζ₃ᶠᶠᶜ(i, j, k, grid, u, v)
end

using Oceananigans.Grids: halo_size, total_size
using Oceananigans.MultiRegion: fill_halo_regions!
using Oceananigans.Utils: KernelParameters, launch!

function compute_vorticity!(grid, u, v, ζ)
    offset = -1 .* halo_size(grid)
    fill_halo_regions!((u, v))
    @apply_regionally begin
        kernel_parameters = KernelParameters(total_size(ζ[1]), offset)
        launch!(arch, grid, kernel_parameters, _compute_vorticity!, grid, u, v, ζ)
    end
end

compute_vorticity!(grid, u, v, ζ)
```
Running this MWE results in a bounds error:
```julia
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access 8×8 OffsetArray(::Matrix{Float64}, -1:6, -1:6) 
with eltype Float64 with indices -1:6×-1:6 at index [-2, -1]
```
Note that the vorticity field `ζ₃ᶠᶠᶜ` at a given grid point is computed by first evaluating the circulation around that point and then dividing it by the corresponding cell area. However, the circulation computation along the outermost line of the western halos `i = 1 - Hx` or the outermost line of the southern halos `j = 1 - Hy` tries to access velocity values beyond their designated halo regions. Specifically, the circulation defined by
```julia
Γ = δxᶠᶠᶜ(i, j, k, grid, Δy_qᶜᶠᶜ, v) - δyᶠᶠᶜ(i, j, k, grid, Δx_qᶠᶜᶜ, u)
```
tries to access `v[i = -Hx]` at `i = 1 - Hx`, and `u[j = -Hy]` at `j = 1 - Hy`, which are out-of-bounds.

Since the vorticity values along the outermost lines of the western and southern halos are not used anywhere, their numerical correctness is not required. So, this PR introduces a simple workaround by clamping the indices used for velocity access when computing vorticity at these halo locations:
```julia
ip = ifelse(i == 1 - Hx, 1, i)
jp = ifelse(j == 1 - Hy, 1, j)
```
These dummy indices `ip` and `jp` are then used in place of `i` and `j` within the vorticity operator, ensuring all array accesses remain within bounds while preserving simulation correctness.
title: Fix out-of-bounds access in vorticity computation at halo boundaries on conformal cubed sphere panels
Try running Langmuir turbulence example on GPU with higher resolution
